message:
test(fcp-core): fixups across operation/tracing/u64-wraparound tests

Three small follow-up edits gathered into one commit because they all
land on the fcp-core test surface and share a single motivation:
keeping the test files self-consistent with rustfmt + the live API.

crates/fcp-core/src/operation.rs:

- Two `assert!(validate_receipt_intent_binding(...)...is_ok())` calls
  in the unit-test block reformatted back to the leading-line-break
  layout that rustfmt picks under our edition settings. The previous
  commit (ede1b1a95) had inadvertently bundled the rustfmt-collapsed
  form alongside the new `canonical_operation_span` API. This restores
  the canonical layout so a future `cargo fmt` run does not produce a
  diff.

crates/fcp-core/tests/tracing_span_contract.rs:

- Import groups re-sorted to the canonical "TypeName...comes first,
  free fns last, with sub-module groups deduplicated and alphabetised"
  order rustfmt is currently set to produce. Affects the `std::sync::*`
  group, the `fcp_core::*` re-export, and the `tracing::*` import. No
  semantic change — same set of names brought into scope.

crates/fcp-core/tests/u64_wraparound_contract.rs:

- Migrate from `CrdtActorId::Node(name.into())` (variant-direct
  construction with implicit `.into::<String>()`) to `CrdtActorId::new(name)`
  (the API constructor). The constructor is the documented entry point
  and gives us a stable signature even if the variant set evolves
  later. Three call sites updated:
  * gcounter_per_actor_increment_saturates_at_u64_max
  * gcounter_aggregate_value_uses_saturating_u128_sum (two sites,
    "alice/bob" cases for both single- and dual-overflow scenarios)

No behavioural changes; tests still assert the same saturating /
checked / saturating policies pinned by the original commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
